meaning: adv.莉､莠ｺ荳榊ｮ牙慍,蜉ｨ鞫勛慍
examples: To a disturbingly large extent, the young and the broke are relying on credit cards to make it until their next payday.
It also has driven food prices disturbingly high.
The notional cost of Irish borrowing shot to disturbingly high levels on Wednesday.
Citing the scientific consensus that global warming is disturbingly real, Francis left little doubt about who to blame.
In short, Mr Berlusconi is more dominant than ever indeed, disturbingly so (see article).
